summary: The ForceHttps middleware reflects the client-provided Host header into the redirect Location without validation. A test binary built against the vulnerable Salvo 0.89.3 code confirms that sending Host: evil-attacker.com produces an HTTP 308 Permanent Redirect to https://evil-attacker.com/, including any query parameters present in the original request URL.
expected_security_impact: An HTTP request with Host: evil-attacker.com should produce a 308 Permanent Redirect response whose Location header contains https://evil-attacker.com/, proving the middleware trusts the unvalidated Host header for redirect URL construction and leaks query parameters to the attacker-controlled domain.
actual_observed_result: Running the test binary against the vulnerable Salvo 0.89.3 code produced: Status 308 Permanent Redirect, Location: https://evil-attacker.com:443/?token=leaked_secret. The attacker-controlled Host header was reflected verbatim in the redirect Location, and query parameters from the original request were leaked to the attacker domain.
verification_method: A Rust test binary using Salvo's TestClient sends a request with Host: evil-attacker.com and query parameter token=leaked_secret to the ForceHttps middleware. The test asserts that (1) the response status is 308 Permanent Redirect and (2) the Location header contains the attacker domain evil-attacker.com and the leaked query parameter. This distinguishes a confirmed open redirect from a normal same-origin redirect or an error response.
evidence_produced_by_run: Test 1: Host: evil-attacker.com -> Status 308 Permanent Redirect, Location: https://evil-attacker.com/
Test 2: Host: evil-attacker.com with ?token=leaked_secret -> Status 308, Location: https://evil-attacker.com:443/?token=leaked_secret
Query parameters from the original request are fully preserved in the redirect to the attacker-controlled domain, confirming information disclosure.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
